deck.title: Build The Demo Judges Can Score

slides.title: Judges cannot score what they cannot verify
slides.subtitle: Build the pitch around the proof they can inspect in the room.

visual_aid.purpose: Open with one memorable operating rule.

speaker_notes.key_message: The deck is about making proof easy to judge, not showing every feature.
talk_track: Start by naming the practical pressure: judges have limited time.
Explain that the team should design for scoring and recall.
Avoid saying this format guarantees a win.
avoid_saying: This is the only way to win a hackathon.

validation.expected: Four rows stay readable on a projector.
validation.fallback: Split into two slides if a real rubric has more categories.

speaker_notes.key_message: The rubric is the first content filter.
talk_track: Devpost and MLH both point to judging as an explicit process.
Do not copy this table blindly; replace it with the event rubric.
Use backup for evidence judges may ask for but do not need in the main story.
delivery_cues: Point to the left column first, then the deck job.

slides.title: Prune by score movement, not effort spent

visual_aid.purpose: Show how a crowded source pile becomes a tight pitch.

validation.expected: Both columns stay balanced with short bullets.
validation.fallback: Move backup examples into speaker notes if cramped.

speaker_notes.key_message: Time is a constraint; backup is a feature, not a failure.
talk_track: Connect short-pitch discipline to the goal of earning the next conversation.
Tell builders to keep proof available without crowding the main story.
Use the one-slide-per-minute rule as a planning default, not a law.

slides.title: Make the demo route reliable
body: Show the route before the live action.
Keep a screenshot, recording, or logs ready if runtime fails.
slides.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Problem] --> B[Input]
  B --> C[System action]
  C --> D[Visible result]
  D --> E[Fallback proof]

visual_aid.purpose: Turn the live demo into a visible sequence.

validation.expected: Mermaid path renders left to right with five nodes or fewer.
validation.fallback: Use the body text as a static route if Mermaid fails.

speaker_notes.key_message: A calm demo plan makes the team look more credible.
talk_track: Name the demo route before clicking anything live.
Explain what judges should watch for.
If the live path breaks, switch to the fallback proof without apologizing for too long.

visual_aid.purpose: Separate useful technical proof from implementation noise.

validation.fallback: Move the last two rows to backup if the real deck adds examples.

speaker_notes.key_message: Technical depth is strongest when it is specific, honest, and inspectable.
talk_track: Explain that technical slides should answer judge questions.
Invite teams to show limitations instead of hiding them.
Project-specific accuracy, speed, cost, and latency need real measurements.
avoid_saying: Our model is best unless you have a measured source.

slides.title: End with the sentence judges can retell
slides.quote: We solve this problem, for this user, with this working proof, and this is the next step.

visual_aid.purpose: Close with a reusable presentation check.

speaker_notes.key_message: The final review should ask whether the idea is clear, credible, and worth the next action.
talk_track: Use the sentence as a rehearsal test.
If the team cannot fill one slot honestly, the idea or evidence needs work.
Move extra technical material to Q&A and backup.
